channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Sunday school class with Jimmy Carter: What it was like
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — It never got old. No matter how many times one crammed into the modest sanctuary at Maranatha Baptist Church, there was always some wisdom to be gleaned from the measured, Bible-inspired words of Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How filmmakers and actors in Asia and the Asian diaspora are expanding representation
item.description: Twenty years after he was a young, struggling actor in Toronto, Thomas Lo is now the one giving young Asian actors their big breaks. He just had to go to Hong Kong to do it.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Soldier who died by suicide in Las Vegas told ex-girlfriend of pain and exhaustion after Afghanistan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The highly decorated Special Forces soldier who died by suicide in a Cybertruck explosion on New Years Day confided to a former girlfriend — who had served as an Army nurse — that he faced significant pain and exhaustion that she says were key symptoms of traumatic brain injury.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: New Los Angeles County DA Nathan Hochman meets with Menendez brothers' family ahead of resentencing bid
item.description: Los Angeles District Attorney Nathan Hochman met with the family of Lyle and Erik Menendez, saying he had a "productive" meeting with the family.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles County DA provides update on meeting with Menendez brothers’ family
item.description: (KTLA) - New Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman held a press conference to provide information on Friday's meeting with about 25 family members of Erik and Lyle Menendez.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: Golden Globes 2025: Who's nominated, hosting, presenting, the honorees, what they'll eat and more
item.description: BEVERLY HILLS, Calif. (AP) — The Golden Globes will return Sunday with major star appeal thanks to a slew of big name nominees, including Zendaya, Timothée Chalamet, Angelina Jolie, Daniel Craig, Denzel Washington, Ariana Grande and more.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The last high-profile trip to India by the Biden administration will focus on technology
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — National security adviser Jake Sullivan will travel to India for a visit focusing on cooperation on strategic technology, including in defense, space and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man hit person with frozen pizza rolls during fight in Walmart checkout line, police say
item.description: A man was arrested on New Year's Eve after he allegedly assaulted two people in a Walmart checkout line, hitting one of them with a bag of frozen pizza rolls, according to police in Conway, South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star Zachery Ty Bryan jailed
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan was arrested this week on a domestic violence charge in South Carolina, online jail records show.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, KRON4

item.title: New videos paint clearer picture of Trump Hotel Cybertruck explosion in Las Vegas
item.description: Two new videos have been released in relation to Wednesday's Cybertruck explosion outside the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas where a former Army soldier died.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly 700 Party City store leases heading to auction
item.description: Nearly 700 Party City store leases will be heading to auction next month, according to an announcement Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, NewsNation

item.title: Rudy Giuliani insists he's not hiding assets at contempt hearing over $148 million judgment
item.description: Rudy Giuliani insisted during three hours of testimony in a New York courtroom at a contempt hearing that he's not hiding assets from lawyers trying to recover a $148 million judgment for two Georgia election workers.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Goldin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled this week the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) lacked the authority to restore certain net neutrality rules last year, handing a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden administration officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to donate funds, blood after fatal Bourbon Street attack
item.description: As the City of New Orleans begins to recover from a fatal attack on Bourbon Street on New Year's Day, efforts are underway to help victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: Bella Dardano, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans' trust in accuracy of US elections jumps after Trump's win, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A majority of Republicans say they are confident in the 2024 vote count after Donald Trump’s win, according to a new poll that finds a sharp turnaround from GOP voters’ skepticism about U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI releases new video of suspect planting pipe bomb on eve of Capitol riot
item.description: A harrowing chapter in American history remains shrouded in mystery: Who planted pipe bombs outside offices of the Democratic and Republican national committees in Washington on the eve of the attack on the Capitol?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Surgeon general calls for cancer warnings on alcohol
item.description: Alcoholic beverages should carry a prominent warning label about the link between alcohol and cancer, according to the U.S. surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven US Army soldiers
item.description: President Biden on Friday awarded the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers, most of whom received it posthumously for service during the Korea War, in his last ceremony as commander-in-chief awarding the country’s highest military honor.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Jill Biden got most expensive gift from a foreign leader in 2023. Here's what it was
item.description: President Joe Biden and his family were given tens of thousands of dollars in gifts from foreign leaders in 2023, according to an annual accounting published by the State Department on Thursday, with first lady Jill Biden receiving the single most expensive present: a $20,000 diamond from India’s le
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 6 times ISIS has inspired terror attacks on US soil
item.description: New Orleans terror suspect Shamsud-Din Jabbar flew an ISIS flag on his truck when he carried out his attack on Wednesday. Here's a list of other ISIS inspired attacks on U.S. soil.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 dead and 19 injured in Southern California plane crash
item.description: FULLERTON, Calif. (AP) — Two people died and 19 were injured Thursday when a small plane crashed through the roof of a sprawling furniture manufacturing building in Southern California where at least 200 people were working, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: How to adjust your budget if you're unemployed
item.description: More than a quarter (26%) of households live "paycheck to paycheck," spending over 95% of their income on necessities.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: NYPD makes arrest in fatal stabbing of on-duty postal worker
item.description: The NYPD has charged a woman in the fatal stabbing inside a Harlem deli of an on-duty USPS worker in broad daylight on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Carter's quest for Mideast peace didn't end with Camp David
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — As president, Jimmy Carter brokered the watershed peace agreement that removed Israel’s most powerful enemy from the battlefield. But he incurred the Israeli government's wrath decades later when he said its military rule over the Palestinians amounted to apartheid.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 killed, 18 injured after plane crashes into warehouse in Southern California
item.description: Two people died and at least 18 people were injured Thursday afternoon when a plane crashed into the roof of a Southern California warehouse.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden awards the 2nd highest civilian award to leaders of the Jan. 6 committee and 18 others
item.description: President Joe Biden on Thursday awarded the second highest civilian medal to Liz Cheney and Bennie Thompson, leaders of the congressional investigation into the Capitol riot who Donald Trump has said should be jailed for their roles in the inquiry.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI says driver in New Orleans rampage acted alone and was '100%' inspired by Islamic State group
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The Army veteran who drove a pickup truck into a crowd of New Year’s revelers in New Orleans acted alone, the FBI said Thursday, reversing its position from a day earlier that he likely worked with others in the deadly attack that officials said was inspired by the Islamic State g
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, JIM MUSTIAN, KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Downtown Omaha loses power for hours after a garbage truck gets stuck in a sinkhole
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A large portion of downtown Omaha lost power for several hours Thursday after a garbage truck got stuck in a sinkhole where utility lines had become exposed. The truck became stuck around 8 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 killed, 18 injured after plane crashes into warehouse in Southern California
item.description: Two people died and at least 18 people were injured Thursday afternoon when a plane crashed into the roof of a Southern California warehouse.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Borrowers cheer Biden's record-setting student loan forgiveness, but left bitter with the system
item.description: While grateful to see their loan balance disappear, borrowers who received forgiveness under Biden lament a system they say is holding back millions of others.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Firework mortars, gas cannisters stuffed inside Tesla that exploded outside Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Firework mortars and camp fuel canisters were found stuffed into the back of the Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump's Las Vegas hotel early Wednesday, killing a suspect inside the vehicle and sparking an intense investigation into possible terrorism.
item.dc:creator: TY O'NEIL and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WGN 9
